jokes.text: Yes. If he's smart, he'll hold out for millions. Whoo-hoo! I got skybox tickets. And with only 20 percent loss of my brain function. Life is... [MUMBLES]
jokes.setup: Homer got injured and Mr. Burns suggests he could sue for millions
jokes.punchline: Homer is excited about skybox tickets despite losing 20% brain function, then demonstrates it by mumbling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - prioritizing immediate gratification over long-term consequences, then physically demonstrating the brain damage

jokes.text: Can't beat a skybox. All the excitement of being in the sky with the security of being in a box.
jokes.setup: Homer describing the appeal of a skybox
jokes.punchline: He literally breaks down the word 'skybox' into its components
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - taking something abstract and making it overly literal

jokes.text: Actually, it says here we're gonna see hockey. [IN UNISON] No! [PEOPLE SCREAMING]
jokes.setup: Family thinks they're going to basketball
jokes.punchline: Universal horror at discovering it's hockey instead
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - the unison reaction and escalation to screaming sells the disappointment

jokes.text: Would you care for some cologne? You have anything with ground-up whale in it? But of course.
jokes.setup: Skybox attendant offers cologne
jokes.punchline: Homer requests whale-based cologne and gets immediate accommodation
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist exchange - Homer's bizarre request and the attendant's matter-of-fact compliance

jokes.text: It's like Moby's house on Cribs.
jokes.setup: Bart describing the fancy skybox
jokes.punchline: Reference to MTV Cribs with electronic musician Moby
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but feels a bit forced for Bart's character

jokes.text: Can you paint me knocking out Larry Holmes? Certainly. Don't hit my face. Okay, where do you want it? I don't know. Work the stomach.
jokes.setup: Homer wants a portrait painted of him boxing Larry Holmes
jokes.punchline: Larry Holmes is actually there and they start discussing where to hit him
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - audience thinks it's about painting, but Larry Holmes is actually there getting beat up

jokes.text: Mm. That takes me back.
jokes.setup: Professional boxer Larry Holmes being asked to work Homer's stomach
jokes.punchline: Holmes nostalgically reminisces about beating people up
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery suggesting Holmes fondly remembers his violent career, great economy of words

jokes.text: Check out those non-skybox losers watching hockey without sushi.
jokes.setup: Homer looking down at regular fans
jokes.punchline: He specifically mentions the lack of sushi as a major deficiency
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's instant class consciousness and food obsession

jokes.text: Let's take Mr. Figgy Pudding down a peg. Otto, what are you waiting for? Get your ass on my neck.
jokes.setup: Barney wants to attack Homer in the skybox
jokes.punchline: He tells Otto to climb on his neck for a human tower assault
jokes.explanation: Absurd nickname for Homer and ridiculous attack strategy perfectly fit these characters

jokes.text: Oh, great. And I just got all that gum out of my armpits.
jokes.setup: Hot fudge has spilled on Barney
jokes.punchline: He recently removed gum from his armpits
jokes.explanation: Perfect gross-out Barney line revealing his hygiene issues

jokes.text: All right. A people tooth. Gummy Sue, this is your lucky day. There. Now I is like Britney Spears.
jokes.setup: A tooth falls from the stands
jokes.punchline: Barney puts it in his mouth and compares himself to Britney Spears
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Barney's excitement over finding a tooth and pop culture reference

jokes.text: This is a joke. You'll find me down with the real fans standing ankle-deep in beer and blood.
jokes.setup: Homer is disappointed with the skybox experience
jokes.punchline: He wants to be with fans standing in beer and blood
jokes.explanation: Homer's fickleness and the visceral imagery of 'ankle-deep in beer and blood' is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Fine. Watch your stupid Eagles concert. It's a hockey game. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Homer is storming off
jokes.punchline: Marge calls it an Eagles concert, showing her disinterest/confusion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's dismissiveness and sports ignorance

jokes.text: Aim for the five hole. He's got an opening the size of Red Square.
jokes.setup: Lisa giving hockey advice
jokes.punchline: Uses Russian reference for hole size when talking to Russian player
jokes.explanation: Smart wordplay connecting Russian player with Red Square metaphor

jokes.text: Thank you, mouthy American child. Please to take my hockey tree.
jokes.setup: Russian hockey player is grateful for Lisa's advice
jokes.punchline: He gives her his 'hockey tree' as a reward
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd concept of a 'hockey tree' and broken English delivery

jokes.text: They've already told the skybox fans who's gonna win.
jokes.setup: Lisa wants to stay for the end of the game
jokes.punchline: Homer implies sports are rigged for VIP customers
jokes.explanation: Good satirical comment on VIP treatment and sports corruption

jokes.text: Because she took the trouble to wander away and talk to a stranger.
jokes.setup: Bart complains about Lisa getting something
jokes.punchline: Homer presents dangerous behavior as commendable
jokes.explanation: Classic bad parenting from Homer, treating stranger danger as a life lesson

jokes.text: Well, cock-a-diddily-doo. What a Marge-alicious way to start my Flander-rific day.
jokes.setup: Flanders greeting the morning
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top alliteration and made-up words in his typical style
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders characterization with his annoying cheerfulness and made-up words

jokes.text: Hey, Ma, our crap shack's going to hell.
jokes.setup: Bart observing their house's condition
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment calling it a 'crap shack'
jokes.explanation: Good Bart line - irreverent and direct about their living situation

jokes.text: These are no ordinary termites. What you've got here are Russian No-wood-niks.
jokes.setup: Exterminator diagnosing the pest problem
jokes.punchline: Made-up Russian termite species name
jokes.explanation: Clever pun combining 'no good niks' with 'no wood' for termites

jokes.text: Okay. But in order to kill these bugs, I've got to live like a bug, think like a bug, become a bug.
jokes.setup: Exterminator explaining his method
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic method actor approach to pest control
jokes.explanation: Funny parody of method acting applied to extermination

jokes.text: I go by how funny the sign is.
jokes.setup: Marge asks why he always hires the cheapest guy
jokes.punchline: Homer's actual selection criteria is humor, not price
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - making important decisions based on entertainment value

jokes.text: I had to use the good poison. It comes with James Coburn's picture on the bottle.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the house will be uninhabitable for six months
jokes.punchline: Quality poison is measured by celebrity endorsement
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept of celebrity-endorsed poison

jokes.text: We'll just hang out in front of the house beside these garbage cans. The time will just fly by.
jokes.setup: Family needs somewhere to live for six months
jokes.punchline: Homer suggests living outside by garbage cans like it's a vacation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer optimism in the face of obvious problems

jokes.text: I'm just gonna say it. We haven't had a good idea since Raisin Bran.
jokes.setup: Bran producers convention
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging their creative bankruptcy since one successful product
jokes.explanation: Funny industry satire about cereal innovation stagnation

jokes.text: I do not want another lecture from a German backpacker about how we don't appreciate the National Park System.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggests a youth hostel
jokes.punchline: Very specific complaint about German tourists
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific and relatable complaint about hostel experiences

jokes.text: That's Lenny? Oh, I wanted the black one.
jokes.setup: Homer says they'll stay with his best friend Lenny
jokes.punchline: Homer admits he confused his friends by race
jokes.explanation: Problematic humor that relies on racial confusion for laughs

jokes.text: Oh, I share a common wall with a jai alai court.
jokes.setup: Explaining the rumbling noise in his apartment
jokes.punchline: Lives next to an obscure sport venue
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random and specific explanation for apartment problems

jokes.text: Ha-ha-ha. That's the sound of the pelota hitting the fronton. I find it soothing.
jokes.setup: Family is terrified by the loud banging
jokes.punchline: Lenny finds the violent sounds relaxing and uses proper jai alai terminology
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between family's terror and Lenny's casual expertise

jokes.text: I'm chairing a meeting of the Galactic U.N. My apologies. The paint is still wet on Ambassador Farfoon.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy hosting the family
jokes.punchline: He's running a sci-fi role-playing meeting with wet paint on figures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy nerdiness with the wet paint detail

jokes.text: We are not staying at Moe's. Maggie's already drunk on the fumes. And she's a mean drunk.
jokes.setup: Moe's tavern as potential lodging
jokes.punchline: Baby is drunk and violent from alcohol fumes
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about baby alcoholism with good character specificity

jokes.text: We can eat the loser. Pfft. Who wants to eat a loser?
jokes.setup: Moe mentions eating the losing cockfight rooster
jokes.punchline: Homer interprets 'loser' metaphorically rather than literally
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that works for Homer's character

jokes.text: 1895? Forget it. We'd be too late to save Lincoln and too early to save Kennedy.
jokes.setup: Reality show set in 1895
jokes.punchline: Homer's timeline of presidential assassinations he could prevent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor connecting historical timeline with personal heroism fantasy

jokes.text: You could save McKinley. It's not a time machine, Moe.
jokes.setup: Moe suggests saving President McKinley
jokes.punchline: Homer has to explain it's not actually time travel
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing characters' confusion about the show concept

jokes.text: Hey, if TV can give Mike O'Malley a home, it can give us one.
jokes.setup: Convincing Homer to do the reality show
jokes.punchline: Reference to TV host Mike O'Malley getting undeserved success
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference that doesn't add much

jokes.text: And by creator, I mean I saw it on Dutch television and tweaked the title.
jokes.setup: TV producer introducing himself as creator
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits to plagiarizing from Dutch TV
jokes.explanation: Good satire of TV industry plagiarism and shamelessness

jokes.text: But isn't the dad Bill Cosby?
jokes.setup: Looking at the Simpson family
jokes.punchline: Mistaking Homer for Bill Cosby
jokes.explanation: Weak visual gag that doesn't really work

jokes.text: Argh! I'll teach you to whistle on the Sabbath!
jokes.setup: Homer in 1895 character
jokes.punchline: Extreme religious anger over minor Sabbath violation
jokes.explanation: Good period-appropriate overreaction from Homer

jokes.text: They go to pieces over nothing. They're perfect for reality TV.
jokes.setup: Producer watching the family's overreaction
jokes.punchline: Identifying dysfunction as ideal TV content
jokes.explanation: Solid satire of reality TV exploitation

jokes.text: Oh, you look adorable, Lisa. Your school chums are going to be so jealous of your little outfit.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on Lisa's period costume
jokes.punchline: Thinks 1895 dress will make modern kids jealous rather than cause mockery
jokes.explanation: Good maternal obliviousness about what kids find cool. Solid character moment showing Marge's disconnect from youth culture.

jokes.text: I look like Buster Brown. Whoever that is.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about his 1895 outfit
jokes.punchline: References character he doesn't actually know
jokes.explanation: Good age-appropriate reference confusion from Bart

jokes.text: Wow, they had an Army helmet under every bed?
jokes.setup: Bart finds something under the bed
jokes.punchline: He mistakes a chamber pot for military equipment
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and innocent misunderstanding

jokes.text: Befoul an Army helmet? You'd like that, wouldn't you, hippie?
jokes.setup: Producer explains it's a chamber pot
jokes.punchline: Homer sees using it as unpatriotic and calls the producer a hippie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer paranoia and misplaced patriotism

jokes.text: Ooh, please be a melon baller. Please be a melon baller.
jokes.setup: Producer about to reveal modern technology in the house
jokes.punchline: Homer hopes for weirdly specific kitchen tool
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random and specific desire from Homer

jokes.text: Uh, ha, ha. My hair isn't really blue.
jokes.setup: Marge in confessional revealing darkest secrets
jokes.punchline: Admits obvious animated character trait as secret
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about cartoon character appearance

jokes.text: Finally. I'm no longer a slave to the Gillette corporation.
jokes.setup: Homer using a straight razor
jokes.punchline: Sees modern shaving as corporate oppression
jokes.explanation: Good anti-corporate paranoia from Homer

jokes.text: Oreos? Sorry, these are from 1896. Non-scarring toilet paper. Ha. Dream on.
jokes.setup: Producer enforcing 1895 rules
jokes.punchline: One-year difference matters, plus toilet paper was dangerous
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of historical accuracy obsession

jokes.text: Urkel-O's. Delicious but forbidden.
jokes.setup: More anachronistic products
jokes.punchline: Steve Urkel-branded cereal that sounds appealing
jokes.explanation: Clever fictional product combining pop culture with breakfast cereal

jokes.text: I don't believe they had those in 1895. Yes, they did. Look closer. Ooh!
jokes.setup: Marge wants tampons
jokes.punchline: Historical tampons are apparently horrifying
jokes.explanation: Simple gross-out humor about historical feminine products

jokes.text: Twenty-three skidoo.
jokes.setup: Producer enforcing period authenticity
jokes.punchline: Uses period-appropriate slang dismissively
jokes.explanation: Period slang usage is mildly amusing but not exceptional

jokes.text: Maybe I could wuther your heights?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be romantic in period language
jokes.punchline: Mixes up 'Wuthering Heights' with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining literature with Homer's romantic clumsiness

jokes.text: Maybe I could wuther your heights?
jokes.setup: Homer attempting period-appropriate seduction
jokes.punchline: Mangled Wuthering Heights reference as pickup line
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer attempt at literary sophistication resulting in mangled classic reference. Good character integration showing his cultural ignorance.

jokes.text: 'Mutt and Jeff' comics are not funny. They're gay, I get it.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about 1895 entertainment
jokes.punchline: Misreads old comic strip as exclusively about homosexuality
jokes.explanation: Period humor misunderstanding, though relies on dated gay stereotypes

jokes.text: Ugh. Bread tastes like clothes. I'm so cold. All of you stink so much.
jokes.setup: Lisa breaking down in confessional
jokes.punchline: Escalating complaints about 1895 living conditions
jokes.explanation: Good breakdown showing Lisa's normally composed character cracking

jokes.text: This is great television. Yeah. I can't wait to see which one of them dies first.
jokes.setup: Executives watching family suffering
jokes.punchline: Casual anticipation of actual death for entertainment value
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark satire of TV industry callousness. Excellent economy in revealing the extreme exploitation mentality.

jokes.text: And when you're on TV, you dig in your claws and you never let go. Just like Bill Meyers.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining TV persistence philosophy
jokes.punchline: References Bill Moyers as example of media tenacity
jokes.explanation: Mild celebrity reference joke that mischaracterizes serious journalist. Functional but relies on audience knowledge of specific media figure.

jokes.text: Telegram for Haywood U. Cuddlemee. Haywood U. Cuddlemee? Big guy in the back, Haywood U. Cuddlemee? Hey-- Oh.
jokes.setup: Delivery boy calling out fake telegram name
jokes.punchline: Name sounds like 'Hey, would you cuddle me?' and Homer falls for it
jokes.explanation: Classic pun setup with good execution and Homer's gullibility

jokes.text: I'm gonna drive a golden spike where your Union meets your Central Pacific. Stop.
jokes.setup: Homer responding to the prank telegram
jokes.punchline: Uses railroad construction metaphor for violence/sex
jokes.explanation: Clever historical metaphor combining railroad history with innuendo

jokes.text: I thought I was regular before, but I was wrong.
jokes.setup: Bart drinking cod liver oil
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about his bowel movements
jokes.explanation: Simple gross-out humor about digestive effects

jokes.text: Oh, I wish you would reconsider the proposal of Hiram Beatwife. He's betrothed to Martha Takeapunch.
jokes.setup: Lisa and Marge in full 1895 character as wealthy family
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous names 'Beatwife' and 'Takeapunch' for potential suitors
jokes.explanation: Good period parody with obviously fake Victorian names

jokes.text: He's betrothed to Martha Takeapunch.
jokes.setup: Discussion of potential marriage arrangements
jokes.punchline: Woman's surname suggests she's prone to violence
jokes.explanation: Simple pun name that's mildly amusing but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Why is the guy from Laverne & Shirley living in our house? Because nobody's watching you clowns.
jokes.setup: Lisa questions why Squiggy has been added to their show
jokes.punchline: Blunt explanation that their show is boring and needs a celebrity boost
jokes.explanation: Good meta commentary on TV desperation moves when shows struggle, brutally honest assessment

jokes.text: If you was to ask me, you're all too calm and happy. The essence of drama is conflict. That's why they gave me this Taser.
jokes.setup: Squiggy explaining why the show is boring
jokes.punchline: Produces a Taser to create artificial conflict
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing reality TV's artificial drama creation

jokes.text: The network made our show more exciting by dumping the house in a river.
jokes.setup: House is floating down river
jokes.punchline: Lisa matter-of-factly explains the absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery explaining the escalating TV madness

jokes.text: This rope was woven from handlebar moustaches.
jokes.setup: Marge throwing Homer a rope
jokes.punchline: Explains its absurd 1895-appropriate construction
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd period-appropriate material that makes no sense

jokes.text: You monsters. You turned this show into a Survivor clone.
jokes.setup: Family facing manufactured survival situation
jokes.punchline: Direct reference to reality TV trend
jokes.explanation: Direct but not particularly clever satire of reality TV evolution

jokes.text: I would never sign that. Unless there was a red sticker that said sign here.
jokes.setup: Producer mentions contract allows anything
jokes.punchline: Homer admits to signing based solely on sticker placement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - ignoring content and following simple visual cues

jokes.text: Do we get lunch? We're not allowed to interfere with the reality of the show. Which reminds me, burn the leftovers. Leave nothing.
jokes.setup: Crew asking about food while maintaining show reality
jokes.punchline: Producer immediately violates policy by ordering evidence destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - immediately contradicts non-interference rule while stating it. Excellent structure showing TV production hypocrisy.

jokes.text: Do we get lunch? We're not allowed to interfere with the reality of the show. Which reminds me, burn the leftovers. Leave nothing.
jokes.setup: Crew asking about lunch during family's crisis
jokes.punchline: Producer denies interference then immediately orders food destruction
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing producer's hypocrisy about interference

jokes.text: We're not savages. I'm a paralegal from Cleveland. And I'm a nutritionist from Santa Fe.
jokes.setup: Homer assumes they're primitive tribe members
jokes.punchline: They reveal modern professional identities
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing reality TV's constructed nature

jokes.text: I just couldn't eat any more kangaroo testicles. The weird part is, now I can't get enough of them.
jokes.setup: Explaining how they lost their reality show
jokes.punchline: Developed Stockholm syndrome for disgusting food
jokes.explanation: Good psychological twist on reality TV challenges

jokes.text: So the show just abandoned us here. The weird part is, now I can't get enough of them.
jokes.setup: Explaining abandonment by TV show
jokes.punchline: Developed addiction to the food that caused initial elimination
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist about acquired taste for disgusting food. Well-structured character development in survival situation.

jokes.text: That's my Homie.
jokes.setup: Homer completely misunderstands the escape plan
jokes.punchline: Marge's resigned affection for his stupidity
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge reaction to Homer's obliviousness

jokes.text: Is it so gosh-darn hard to get cocktail sauce in the middle of the Amazon?
jokes.setup: Producer eating in jungle location
jokes.punchline: Complaining about condiment availability in remote location
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing producer's entitled attitude

jokes.text: Now we know what it feels like to be tormented. And yet I learn nothing.
jokes.setup: Family has been through traumatic reality TV experience
jokes.punchline: Homer acknowledges learning opportunity but rejects it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-awareness combined with stubborn refusal to grow - excellent character comedy

jokes.text: Law & Order: Elevator Inspectors Unit.
jokes.setup: Family trying to watch quality TV
jokes.punchline: Absurd Law & Order spinoff about elevator inspection
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of TV franchise oversaturation

jokes.text: TV was the one good thing in my life and now I can't enjoy it any more.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes TV is ruined for him
jokes.punchline: Admits TV was literally his only joy in life
jokes.explanation: Sad but funny admission about Homer's priorities and limited interests

jokes.text: Hmm. The water's off. I'll use my eye to see what the trouble is. [HOMER YELLS]
jokes.setup: Homer investigating hose problem
jokes.punchline: Gets water sprayed directly in his eye
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup though fairly predictable

jokes.text: So this has become a game of wits, has it? Perhaps my other eye will get to the bottom of this.
jokes.setup: Homer got sprayed in one eye
jokes.punchline: Treats it as intellectual challenge and offers up his other eye
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Homer's stubborn pride and complete lack of learning - perfectly in character
